description_long: This is a medium-sized, open-sided passenger boat with a flat roof canopy and a catamaran-style twin hull. The hulls are predominantly white with red-orange trim along the upper edges, and the roof is a solid light gray. The sides of the boat display logos and text, including 'Yapha Diving Resorts' and a turtle conservation center, suggesting it is used for diving excursions or eco-tours. The interior is open and accessible, with visible seating and handrails, and the stern features a central boarding platform for easy water access. The boat appears to be constructed mainly from fiberglass or composite materials, with metal supports for the canopy and seating. The overall design is functional and robust, suitable for transporting groups of people on water in urban or resort environments.
description: A white and red-orange catamaran-style passenger boat with a flat canopy roof, open sides, and visible seating, used for diving or eco-tours.
description_view_0: Front view showing the twin hulls, open bow area, and the flat canopy roof.
description_view_1: Side view displaying the full length of the boat, logos, text, and open passenger area under the canopy.
description_view_2: Rear view highlighting the central boarding platform, twin hulls, and open stern access.
description_view_3: Opposite side view with similar features to view 1, including logos and open seating area.
